title: Tender Tamarind Dutch Chicken Stew
description: A hearty and kid-friendly Dutch stew made with tender chicken, tamarind pulp, and a hint of butter, all cooked to perfection in a copper pot. This dish is kosher-friendly and features a delicious salt-bake flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the chicken thighs with salt and black pepper.
Melt the butter in the copper pot over medium heat.
Add the chicken thighs to the pot and brown them on all sides.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes to the pot.
Pour in the chicken broth and add the tamarind pulp to the pot.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake the stew for 45 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the stew cool for a few minutes before serving.
